examples: She set up a literacy program

meanings.definition: set up or lay the groundwork for
establish the validity of something, as by an example, explanation or experiment
examples: The experiment demonstrated the instability of the compound
The mathematician showed the validity of the conjecture
Her manager had set her up at the Ritz

meanings.definition: use as a basis for; found on
examples: base a claim on some observation

meanings.definition: brought about or set up or accepted; especially long established
examples: a team established as a member of a major league
enjoyed his prestige as an established writer
that smoking causes health problems is an accomplished fact
a conventional view of the world

meanings.definition: shown to be valid beyond a reasonable doubt
examples: the established facts in the case

meanings.definition: introduced from another region and persisting without cultivation
